growth_records.medium: Modified N-free Hoagland solution + 20 mM glucose, with iron plaque (Fe(III)) as terminal electron acceptor

up.method: reported initial glucose; CCE ~94.3% of consumed glucose carbon entered energy-producing pathways under Fe(III) plaque

sec.units: % of product carbon (carbon conversion efficiency, relative to glucose consumed)
sec.method: reported carbon fraction, Fig 4b / Suppl Table S2
reported; ferrozine assay, Fe(II) rose from undetectable to 2.11-2.24 mM

sec.compound: lactate, acetone, ethanol, butanol, formate, CO2, H2 (also detected end-products)
sec.method: qualitatively reported as major/minor products; individual quantitative values only in Supplementary Table S2 (not in article XML)

growth_records.cite: Zhan et al. Iron plaques as terminal electron acceptors optimize clostridial fermentation and nitrogen fixation in rice rhizospheres. The ISME Journal (2026). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrag088. [PMC13134045]
growth_records.snippet: acidogenesis predominated in the presence of Fe(III) plaque, with butyrate accounting for 33.2% of carbon products compared to 20.8% in its absence. Acetate showed a similar increase (4.9% with Fe(III) plaque vs. 3.7% without) ... glucose o
growth_records.notes: Iron-plaque (IP) condition. No growth rate/doubling time reported; strain grown to mid-log/steady state. Secretion values are CARBON FRACTIONS (% of product carbon relative to glucose consumed, per equation CCE=sum(C_metabolite x nC)/(C_glucose x 6)x100), not mmol/gDW/h. butyrate 33.2%, acetate 4.9%. Glucose 20 mM initial (batch). Fe(II) produced 2.11 +/- 0.06 to 2.24 +/- 0.17 mM by day 6 (ferrozine). glucose oxidation efficiency 22.2% (fraction of theoretical 24 mol e-/mol glucose); CCE ~94.3%. Nitrogenase activity 2006.19 +/- 64.89 nmol C2H4 mg protein-1 h-1 (acetylene reduction), and 1322.16 vs 1044.24 nmol in microcosm comparison. Full per-product mol/mol-glucose fluxes are in Supplementary Table S2, not present in XML. medium_composition_stated false because full Hoagland salt concentrations not given (only glucose 20 mM, pH 6.0).

growth_records.medium: Modified N-free Hoagland solution + 20 mM glucose, no iron plaque (control, CK)

sec.units: % of product carbon (carbon conversion efficiency, relative to glucose consumed)

sec.compound: lactate, acetone, ethanol, butanol, formate, CO2, H2 (also detected end-products)
sec.method: qualitatively reported; quantitative values only in Supplementary Table S2 (not in XML)

growth_records.cite: Zhan et al. Iron plaques as terminal electron acceptors optimize clostridial fermentation and nitrogen fixation in rice rhizospheres. The ISME Journal (2026). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrag088. [PMC13134045]
growth_records.snippet: butyrate accounting for 33.2% of carbon products compared to 20.8% in its absence. Acetate showed a similar increase (4.9% with Fe(III) plaque vs. 3.7% without Fe(III) plaque).
growth_records.notes: Plaque-free control (CK) condition. No growth rate/doubling time reported. Secretion values are CARBON FRACTIONS (% of product carbon), not mmol/gDW/h: butyrate 20.8%, acetate 3.7% (both lower than IP condition, consistent with more solventogenesis). Glucose 20 mM initial (batch). Under control, more solventogenic/unmetabolized products (hexose phosphate, pyruvate) accumulate; glucose oxidation efficiency and CCE lower than IP. Nitrogenase activity lower without plaque (e.g. 1695.49 +/- 108.12 nmol C2H4 mg protein-1 h-1; 1044.24 nmol in microcosm). Full per-product fluxes in Supplementary Table S2 (not in XML).
